macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 Breaks Major Support Level, 10% Decline Likely
items.summary: The S&P 500 closed at 6,673 on Thursday, below its December low of 6,720, breaking major support amid rising put-call ratios and weak market breadth signaling bearish trends for stocks.

items.title: Dow Jones on Brink of Bearish Breakdown Below 200-Day Moving Average
items.summary: The DJIA, down 4.7% since late February amid US-Iran war escalation, risks breaking key support at 46,330 near its 200-day MA, becoming one of the worst-performing US indices.

items.title: US Stock Futures Jump to Cap Volatile Week
items.summary: US stock futures rose Friday morning with S&P 500 up 0.45%, Nasdaq 0.43%, and Dow 0.41%, amid a rowdy week influenced by war realities, oil prices, and shifting market sentiment.

items.title: S&P 500 and Nasdaq Reverse Gains After Economic Data Releases
items.summary: Both the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 turned lower on Friday following a wave of economic data, contributing to ongoing market declines.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: President Trump Demands Immediate Fed Rate Cut Amid Iran Conflict and Surging Oil Prices
items.summary: President Trump urged Fed Chair Jerome Powell to cut interest rates immediately, skipping the March FOMC meeting, as global economy faces pressure from Iran tensions and oil prices nearing $100 per barrel. This move is linked to boosting Bitcoin amid market volatility.

items.title: Bitcoin Holds Steady as Oil Prices Spike 9% to $96 WTI and Brent Tops $100 on Hormuz Strait Tensions
items.summary: Oil prices surged with WTI up 9% to $96 and Brent over $100 due to Iran's new hardline leader threatening continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz. Bitcoin is relatively resilient compared to stock drops like TQQQ falling 5%.

items.title: Crypto Market Analysts Predict Bitcoin Breakout and Wave 3 Rally Despite Bearish Flags
items.summary: Multiple analysts forecast a huge Bitcoin breakout and Wave 3 parabolic move, citing bear flag patterns, ETF inflows, and end of bear market signals, even as stocks like S&P 500 test lows amid oil disruptions.

items.title: USDT Dominance Poised for Upside Move Aligning with Bitcoin Sentiment Recovery
items.summary: USDT dominance is grinding up and expected to rise soon, in line with improving Bitcoin sentiment, increased exchange volume, and positive ETF inflows signaling potential market rotation.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: SAP Labs Announces Partnership to Hire and Train AI Scientists and Machine Learning Engineers
items.summary: SAP Labs Singapore partners with IMDA to hire and train AI scientists and ML engineers, enhancing enterprise AI innovation including generative and agentic AI capabilities embedded in SAP's cloud applications. This initiative supports Singapore's AI ambitions and global AI product roadmap.

items.title: GPT-5.4 Released, Marking Major Advancement in AI Models
items.summary: OpenAI's GPT-5.4 makes a significant splash in the AI landscape, alongside trends like AI growth on mobile devices, off-grid data centers, and Apple's diffusion research. This release highlights ongoing rapid progress in large language models.

items.title: Northwestern University Launches New AI Major Focused on Machine Learning and Societal Impact
items.summary: Northwestern's McCormick School of Engineering introduces a new AI major emphasizing machine learning, natural language processing, and the societal implications of AI. Faculty and students anticipate it will prepare students for industry demands.

items.title: MIT Experts Discuss Future of AI in Mathematical and Physical Sciences
items.summary: MIT highlights the bidirectional relationship between AI and sciences, with AI advancing research in particle physics and scientific methods improving AI understanding. Initiatives like IAIFI and A3D3 are driving interdisciplinary AI-science collaborations.

items.title: AI Redefining Entry-Level Tech Roles, Urging IT Leaders to Reskill Workforce
items.summary: AI is transforming entry-level tech jobs, prompting CIOs to re-engineer roles and address 'experience starvation' by pairing human talent with AI. Analysts warn of potential talent shortages without reskilling 700,000 US workers by 2027.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 Wins Game of the Year at GDC Awards 2026
items.summary: The French-made role-playing game dominated the Game Developers Choice Awards, taking home Game of the Year plus four additional awards including Best Debut, Best Visual Art, Best Audio, and Best Narrative.

items.title: Lightspeed Studios Unveils Proprietary Development Framework for Triple-A Games
items.summary: The PUBG Mobile co-developer and Tencent subsidiary announced a new IP development framework prioritizing visual identity and art direction, with details being shared at GDC Festival of Gaming.

items.title: Xsolla Releases Industry Report on Video Game Development Opportunities
items.summary: The report reveals AI is compressing development timelines by 20% and reducing support costs by 40%, while 43% of US gamers now play across multiple platforms, and live service titles continue to grow.

items.title: Sirius Dice Signs Licensing Deal with Middle-earth Enterprises
items.summary: At GAMA Expo 2026, Sirius Dice announced an official licensing agreement to produce The Lord of the Rings games accessories.

items.title: Super Mario Galaxy Movie Casts Donald Glover as Yoshi
items.summary: The upcoming film announced additional cast members including Luis Guzman as Wart and Issa Rae as Honey Queen.

items.title: Nintendo and Lego Reveal New Luigi Mario Kart Set for Mar10 Day
items.summary: The Luigi-themed Lego Mario Kart set was announced as part of Nintendo's Mar10 Day celebrations.

items.title: Chainsaw Man Part 2 Manga Ending in Two Weeks
items.summary: Tatsuki Fujimoto's Chainsaw Man manga part 2 will conclude in approximately two weeks, surprising fans of the series.

items.title: Summer Game Fest 2026 Officially Announced
items.summary: The annual gaming event has officially announced its 2026 schedule with details on expected game announcements.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Motional Robotaxis Now Available via Uber App in Las Vegas
items.summary: Hyundai-owned Motional has integrated its autonomous Ioniq 5 robotaxis into the Uber app for rides in five Las Vegas areas, with safety monitors present. This launch follows a major company reset, $1 billion investment from Hyundai, and a pivot to neural network-based AV technology.[2]

items.title: Major Tech Companies Pledge Self-Supplied Power for AI Data Centers
items.summary: Amazon, Google, Meta, Microsoft, Oracle, OpenAI, and xAI have signed a federal pledge to provide their own power sources for AI data centers to avoid straining consumer electricity supplies.[1]

items.title: Adobe Earnings Hit by Decline in Traditional Stock Photo Segment
items.summary: Adobe beat earnings expectations but faces challenges in its stock photo business due to competition from AI-generated imagery, leading to lower annual recurring revenue in that segment.[3]

items.title: Multiple Aerospace Firms Set Up Operations in Swindon
items.summary: At least five companies, including Stark, Tekever, DSEI Flyby, Munin Dynamics, and Neros, are operating or planning to locate in Swindon, boosting the local tech and aerospace sector.[5]
